manual_boundaries: online source bodies are summarized and hashed; public artifacts do not store full page bodies
screenshots/frames are research replay evidence, not trading authority
credentials and API keys are not read
external websites are fetched through ordinary GET requests only; no form submission or account mutation

manual_boundaries: metacognition classifies and routes research context only
missing evidence remains visible as unknown rows
generated code must pass tests before it influences confidence
this packet cannot place trades, read credentials, or mutate external services

organism_route_rows.relation: reads source strength, freshness, contradiction, and confidence context
reads friction, feasibility, urgency, and risk pressure from research context
maps harmonic coherence and repeatability into explainable HNC score features
publishes research understanding phases for whole-organism visibility
receives learning memory, route confidence, and validation pressure
tracks generated helper code and handoff files as authored research artifacts
validates the generated model before it influences confidence
may consume calibrated features later, but this packet cannot place orders

metacognition.query: harmonic nexus score phi signal coherence validation

test_action_rows.purpose: prove the generated score helper is deterministic and bounded
make Seer/Lyra/HNC consume the score as explainable context only
measure whether the score improves future decisions before promotion

metacognition.understanding_summary: Aureon understands Harmonic Nexus Score as a source-linked research packet with 8/8 concept rows and 7/8 organism routes ready. It is routed as evidence, learning, code, and validation context, not as direct trading authority.

unknown_rows.next_action: compare score changes against real outcome evidence before raising confidence
re-fetch original URLs when deeper review is needed instead of storing public page bodies

source_rows.excerpt: Discrete Fourier Transform — NumPy v2.4 Manual Skip to main content Back to top Ctrl + K Choose version GitHub Discrete Fourier Transform # The SciPy module scipy.fft is a more comprehensive superset of numpy.fft , which includes only a basic set of routines. Standard FFTs # fft (a[, n, axis, norm, out]) Compute the one-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform
source_rows.summary: rfftn (a[, s, axes, norm, out]) Compute the N-dimensional discrete Fourier Transform for real input. irfft2 (a[, s, axes, norm, out]) Computes the inverse of rfft2 . rfft2 (a[, s, axes, norm, out]) Compute the 2-dimensional FFT of a real array.
source_rows.title: Discrete Fourier Transform — NumPy v2.4 Manual

source_rows.excerpt: Signal processing (scipy.signal) — SciPy v1.17.0 Manual Skip to main content Back to top Ctrl + K Choose version GitHub Scientific Python Forum Search Ctrl + K Signal processing ( scipy.signal ) # Convolution # convolve (in1, in2[, mode, method]) Convolve two N-dimensional arrays. correlate (in1, in2[, mode, method]) Cross-correlate two N-dimensional arrays.
source_rows.summary: qspline1d (signal[, lamb]) Compute quadratic spline coefficients for rank-1 array. cspline1d (signal[, lamb]) Compute cubic spline coefficients for rank-1 array. B-splines # gauss_spline (x, n) Gaussian approximation to B-spline basis function of order n.
source_rows.title: Signal processing (scipy.signal) — SciPy v1.17.0 Manual

source_rows.excerpt: Signal-to-noise ratio - Wikipedia Jump to content From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia Ratio of the desired signal to the background noise "Signal-to-noise" redirects here. For statistics, see Effect size . For other uses, see Signal-to-noise (disambiguation) . Not to be confused with Signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio . A gray-scale photograph with di
source_rows.summary: [ 17 ] See also [ edit ] Audio system measurements Generation loss Matched filter Near–far problem Noise margin Omega ratio Pareidolia Peak signal-to-noise ratio Signal-to-noise statistic Signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio SINAD SINADR Subjective video quality Total harmonic distortion Video quality Notes [ edit ] ^ The connection between optical power and voltage in an imaging system is linear. A high SNR means that the signal is clear and easy to detect or interpret, while a low SNR means that the signal is corrupted or obscured by noise and may be difficult to distinguish or recover. SNR is an important parameter that affects the performance and quality of systems that process or transmit signals, such as communication systems , audio equipment , radar systems , imaging systems , and data acquisition systems.
source_rows.title: Signal-to-noise ratio - Wikipedia
